reason: Response B leans heavily on the saccharine, cliche-stacked register ("unconditional love," "memories that will last a lifetime," "love, laughter") that imitators tend to overproduce when mimicking a warm assistant, with uniform polish and little texture. Response A has more idiosyncratic, grounded phrasing and a slightly imperfect human cadence ("that is the thing having happened," "the unglamorous core," "Welcome to it"), the kind of restrained, concrete warmth that reads as the genuine model rather than a stylized parody of it. The odd-but-deliberate constructions in A are harder to fake than B's smooth greeting-card flow.
